Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is our fastest and most efficient Gemini 3 series  
model<https://c.gle/ADMV5J-0RmbWcBubMyYdFLqPfPO4uRw1_jvCQ5iYYDfvpfOnl9HzwEH-2K0imw33p7hArs1ljey9_DQD5rSrEBmA2CcnjsEdbBogp_pdKRsIKAftC0QVb6xmTjdAq45wVlz5GAe0FWa93PLXF1Ct5KMC9OTBpMN-BubtFO91RHAVleMSBoBk4QsDBWOpRNIA2vHhZe9OlTuIA51pY5-LdIwiPnHh7YB2sA>

Build better with the fast and cost-efficient Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite.

This excellent Gemini 3 series model is designed to give you tremendous  
performance at a fraction of the cost of larger models. It’s great at  
reasoning and multimodal understanding. Try it out in AI Studio and Vertex  
AI.

Learn  
more<https://c.gle/ADMV5J9zl3JIqjUxyj75dwaYsiNuIhjj_TWTzFCN_z1AJ2vCmSwilFyR80OJHqsj5tMjubCErHFukRndqN7ZFc8PzrtnoY6mlxpfcNqff0Nd9BDURbzEeuBb3IGutHs1eGyEstzfwZJEx4BOnjTueal4qTCRJT4R42h4GfDPDP7S3FalrX9K1FdTmXf-vdGyeGed4Ew8Ht8Avh4DCzVITWQfJb03jPzvJw>

Google offers many MCP servers including fully managed ones and new open  
ones like this one from  
Colab<https://c.gle/ADMV5J_dzN_iehfjnUA5UfZeG-fcXUlrjyu1ONr6lUT_s02lPRfbm3fA5XYD4Piw77cDAGrnwxwMThogSpjEC5g5nNqdTwW80_qdGBfqL556Al5wM9GJd_1atxxxUBGizE-rdCFS1vv95bin3BTiscV-auU8ByPC4zetzaDRCPO94aXeIC-T8U2aIZEKZKrwxmAib3u7O06To8nKJk6FyjrsVdGx8tG-9OZdsE69_Ad6bA>

Build smarter with MCP servers from Google.

Did you know that Google offers over a dozen fully managed MCP servers so  
that your tools and agents can more easily “talk” to their favorite  
services?

Whether Google  
Maps<https://c.gle/ADMV5J9s-DrXo6jcsErlh6d2GWK5AdqVniHYRd4vD8sdADvs5vnUUlEE1AN-at3bABI_ye7aUDxObL4npvnNnBt4xe7DgYnhd5Wu2-eGoiiB3qus68UQsgx0ljDJ_4PoWNuGrUJJwE4WKBdJv7jx4nMHmfIx5Lsd3CfRQHsveA>  
or Cloud  
services<https://c.gle/ADMV5J91IpLKCzqciuZUsniwLQisKVeKy2zslgaUnwv_nArglXr5XOJVDEoyLpvyKGHj_50oSQXU8NYZg42jwgtTS2gsxY3j5HR_MCxwxFntK2JHN3NnVC0Tmchj8dAROAskFGJmmIRarpi38tRdK8R1SSllM0WSxR3i-FiNPS35YlmBTpNJW_tvzQbYbFbXsT9LECH7b4sKEoc>  
like BigQuery, Firestore, Vertex AI, and Cloud Run, you get easy access to  
safe, available endpoints. And don’t miss the new open source Colab MCP  
server<https://c.gle/ADMV5J8lHeop2ypKM84mlrUnILzXMPFiIZZj_Y2h1M2h7CpB95FupEKYI3abOjw2Inn2wslM_uGm9iaxsPbmlfxwC96-xJsWCHw0HygiB-xEal34heYYFYUh0MKZrDnJpAw-N-9SofK0FecJe4zXnrm7ymcFV2HvsxQzqQP37v2YzwjYI6fEcJvAhek5R5hPRwDuTSVRpv5cXq9KNlXsx7r3c37j2RtpMuyjet9E-oFmrTRmX9H_JQSUYju-SZRA>  
that lets you treat notebooks as tools!

Googler Aron Eidelman explains how to build production AI agents that are  
protected and responsible with  
data<https://c.gle/ADMV5J9eOLfkrAji0wPyLQx4jx-fBfaV8jnqV5V0Sq33GRW7IYG3cQ-gkK137JfDnuoCEljkVauKEzZwIgQS2eqs2VlTtDA6E80OAhnhVOz-07gI5h85yKP6m7Uw5JkpUJntnSV06bXbXQrFWLsP1u9xC_pQcg>

Build safer with ADK and Antigravity.

Aron’s got a great video that explains what it means to protect your custom  
agents with products like Model Armor, while preventing data leakage and  
other risks. See how the Agent Development Kit and Google Antigravity help  
along the way.

Learn  
more<https://c.gle/ADMV5J_nf_1vtoh5uClvfcs0_TQH0jIse8a_8QFvGZSpEwn48-fymkTzOQivQ33Gh5Q-FLcuZ27t29kpEvW2Dk4EZGhm1xunw5VYSaxo61zL4oxuHWT0SZ4uRwMZ90d5ISCnjeiT1Lo9XVLE8Ct7goF1NlWdjw>

FAST FIVE

[Devices] Now Available: New Support for Setup & Reset Instructions for  
Matter Devices.

You can now provide a tailored setup experience for your Matter Plugs and  
Lights within the Google Home app. Use the Developer Console to upload  
step-by-step instructions and visual assets (like LED pulse animations) for  
Setup and Reset modes. This  
update<https://c.gle/ADMV5J8lF491i1aCA733qz6qnYQiLKvgY2D0gFaY5d712QcUqP8hfV6L_WbQm-1aWCVoVwyg2w-RnPm2Jgj229Q8d6iEi7UPpyBjOv8KLK2k_R5eHKKVAODOurCJpnwBUZsaU_x1ZVUrkh4O4igPjbvjmgK6-fkl_Tai-MYlDwgtLgt1DjU>  
helps users confirm device behavior in real-time, leading to a 10% increase  
in commissioning success rates.

[Cloud] Secure your Cloud Run apps with on-click IAP integration.

Identity-aware proxy (IAP) gives you control over who can access your  
application, whether that’s inside or outside of your organization. You can  
now enable this for Cloud Run  
services<https://c.gle/ADMV5J_o4B2Ibvkx5XcI7rO5SkmT80BT7QMUgWsC6RPXxNV7uAyeVnIRSuQuaNJBjCyOQ8IgUz3sM1K4WpyfHhTxHb2zYhIHIxAfCSWxqLgwAoq2LwHyLqojokHsyLGUKRIoFFUji34KE8hTAlQRQv5axLQlRjPuDpftPm66DfMej2j07_Skcc0IB9DU3emk3Hpb1Y3wRHIk5aZRP2PfHtyBIvYjNmEgMif8kP_ZNKdCDG9r>  
with one-click in the console, a single gcloud CLI flag, or one Terraform  
configuration.

[Android] Evaluate LLM capabilities for Android development with Android  
Bench.

We just delivered the first version of this leaderboard of LLMs for Android  
development. We used real-world Android development scenarios to ensure we  
had a realistic assessment. Check out the first  
results!<https://c.gle/ADMV5J9SbKKQKEfd9OaZ_M4DhpZqxC2IKBYepupwqKuAUpqqHKfEIzNrMDDUTXC3eHaW-ymVqyk94Sv-AUQdfRSW7wEYTVwqfueWnbYQCr38TekXw71tVGUblDW0pa_p6_WlAlqn5L8WgSGTej1h46CTIxyHxY1hn1UUdx3Rcz84GUpA3nHDAuslwBBQbDIG1KVR0R0ckY53Dlpyhi3Ne-W7SgYPEILbMIhZ>

[Firebase] Firebase A/B testing now available for the web.

Run, analyze, and scale experiments with Firebase A/B testing. This popular  
capability for mobile devs is now available for those building Firebase web  
apps  
too.<https://c.gle/ADMV5J-HB3RoEKiH9oAYVcbbA-1oXJa41zSzHi1FEU_9muh9lkScEBbF9ACY5rXcuJ5lThNP5jYszcy5ZgYDJLhGSWPn59qUPys2pcYK8wDdUTovquvNKtqTwoqw23jjQv5m_ZAkBNetUWWho9VJvudJWe4PW5vmDN0XLxngu1E0bJhHJ4Gj0qSISddFlsF9nSK_DnaZpZ_dUmWFPwWNU1wE9QHd4w>

[Stitch] Create and iterate on designs using natural language in the  
upgraded Stitch.

I really like using this service. And now it’s even better. This product  
evolution makes it easier to explore ideas quickly and work within a new  
infinite canvas to iterate on your work. This, and much more in the latest  
update.<https://c.gle/ADMV5J8pjiPBEkNfzDYRSr2Y6Bs92wwq9KH2ycIySGwKoAQngWWQPPFWe2MpopoTei_paPr2zh2PNYgvD9f1osbNgdSPDVFOI7M4hexgdmqWvREejHzSWnJDQUlKayhsRkat6jY1F6acTQxDiGU74jN3T_YBaUMZ0JS4xl6DSCN-20xz3ECut6Yuo5xnH5a95Ef0k3HHF-Cieok8rP08EiJENbvSTzgUDMY>

LEVEL UP WITH LEARNING

Go from design-to-code with Antigravity and Stitch

Take a look at this brand-new codelab which teaches us how to build  
high-fidelity UIs in Stitch, and connect them to Antigravity using the  
Stitch MCP server.

Start  
learning<https://c.gle/ADMV5J80bVCfx13PXaTHdXEToyTQzhczWy4-LB9DO4U_SQC4PCUV5SmFML-qqZOCFQKuR2T4arab1wf03i6fHdLeHerjpGGgwpdEc72CEF2WzSqoZkTJiYjHFcs7jsAaSCgkJ-mBvx0-fHMm9Vji_LKm1OW0WFBMmm7lAWKd2cxzBVvUWvnA6hwmK_v5dmXJ9s08tymgNMzi-PPl7ZB3v9dQ5g>

Read the developer’s guide to AI agent protocols

Can’t tell your MCP from your AP2? Don’t feel bad, it’s a lot to keep up  
with. Take advantage of posts like this one that breaks down the core  
protocols that matter for AI agent developers. It includes code samples for  
each!

Read  
more<https://c.gle/ADMV5J9Kzq6N-cW8HVze_7OpAQXOoHFaYGJhA9dZAZ8q_s2VdGHm6UNtOm5nZlcxnKgZDwItFYEKLTATx3leF9wI5S5IjqQL8Q3VjUegZbazkK3CyDfOTdCw_fFkDztniakDPQH_yg31JXwFMOsVPpdmaVLnIWrrlCf4Kl2g2logkPxv0c30jl4IP6NXogUZcYQ>

DEVELOPER COMMUNITY

Angular community experts share what they’re learning

Many of us are not just changing what we build, but how we build. This  
roundup of resources and examples from Angular community experts may  
inspire you to try some new patterns.

Read the  
article<https://c.gle/ADMV5J_1jHL8iLnPAiuz8qiQegjxnBtYo96c5pmE4Yh52Uqfg5R4e0_skZze2D4TKirjadBKAW-7voIQWivB3NR4fbbB4oxcodFav3gJC12lbzOupVqPfkJqcJPLtHdOOW-bmNTgQNkWsIH7xgvwQZnI9xOcce7YtyFAIq65N67cpSs4rCy0TjvQU7S7IB4gGwJgeCrE-tskiXxBV3VAWTFRTGx8YgK1KIF9-UWWj2sU3GOEKcZVhfrGcSMw49YCsRgqvEauJic>

Query images and videos directly in BigQuery

Is there anything BigQuery can’t do? I’m always amazed by its power and  
flexibility. This post explores the way to query structured,  
semi-structured, and unstructured data—thanks BigLake Object  
Tables!—without leaving BigQuery.

Read the

decision.reason: Existing smart unit does not cover enough of the inferred requirement capabilities.

explanations.router: Use the lightest local model for always-on intent triage.
explanations.semantic: Keep semantic normalization local to reduce latency and preserve privacy.
explanations.planner: Promote to a cloud planner only when the task needs network-backed deep planning or the cortex is highly specialized.
explanations.executor: Keep the main execution loop local-first so HomeHub behaves like a persistent box brain.
explanations.vision: Use local VL for default document/image understanding, and cloud multimodal only when richer network inference is allowed.
explanations.artifact: Route artifact and workflow generation to the coder model unless the task explicitly benefits from stronger cloud synthesis.
explanations.response: Use a smaller cloud model for polished responses only when the task already depends on the network.
explanations.embedding: Prefer multilingual embeddings for repo-brain memory and cross-lingual retrieval.
explanations.reranker: Rerank retrieved evidence before the exec-brain consumes it.
explanations.similarity: Use a compact multilingual similarity model to find matching brains, commands, and memory shards.
explanations.docMemory: Use multimodal memory indexing when the request contains images or document evidence.
explanations.audioReasoner: Reserve audio-text-to-text models for future richer voice reasoning instead of plain STT.

preBrain.name: pre-brain
preBrain.goal: Convert raw command, voice, and image signals into one semantic command packet.

stages.label: Language Classifier
stages.purpose: Normalize zh/en/ja input and decide the semantic lane.

stages.label: Semantic Normalizer
stages.purpose: Unify text, STT text, and OCR text into one command object.

execBrain.name: exec-brain
execBrain.goal: Judge demand fit, reuse or create the right smart units autonomously, compile a taskflow, and execute it.

Build smarter with MCP servers from Google.

or Cloud  
services<https://c.gle/ADMV5J91IpLKCzqciuZUsniwLQisKVeKy2zslgaUnwv_nArglXr5XOJVDEoyLpvyKGHj_50oSQXU8NYZg42jwgtTS2gsxY3j5HR_MCxwxFntK2JHN3NnVC0Tmchj8dAROAskFGJmmIRarpi38tRdK8R1SSllM0WSxR3i-FiNPS35YlmBTpNJW_tvzQbYbFbXsT9LECH7b4sKEoc>  
like BigQuery, Firestore, Vertex AI, and Cloud Run, you get easy access to  
safe, available endpoints. And don’t miss the new open source Colab MCP  
server<https://c.gle/ADMV5J8lHeop2ypKM84mlrUnILzXMPFiIZZj_Y2h1M2h7CpB95FupEKYI3abOjw2Inn2wslM_uGm9iaxsPbmlfxwC96-xJsWCHw0HygiB-xEal34heYYFYUh0MKZrDnJpAw-N-9SofK0FecJe4zXnrm7ymcFV2HvsxQzqQP37v2YzwjYI6fEcJvAhek5R5hPRwDuTSVRpv5cXq9KNlXsx7r3c37j2RtpMuyjet9E-oFmrTRmX9H_JQSUYju-SZRA>

protected and responsible with  
data<https://c.gle/ADMV5J9eOLfkrAji0wPyLQx4jx-fBfaV8jnqV5V0Sq33GRW7IYG3cQ-gkK137JfDnuoCEljkVauKEzZwIgQS2eqs2VlTtDA6E80OAhnhVOz-07gI5h85yKP6m7Uw5JkpUJntnSV06bXbXQrFWLsP1u9xC_pQcg>

agents with products like Model Armor, while preventing data leakage and  
other risks. See how the Agent Development Kit and Google Antigravity help  
along the way.

more<https://c.gle/ADMV5J_nf_1vtoh5uClvfcs0_TQH0jIse8a_8QFvGZSpEwn48-fymkTzOQivQ33Gh5Q-FLcuZ27t29kpEvW2Dk4EZGhm1xunw5VYSaxo61zL4oxuHWT0SZ4uRwMZ90d5ISCnjeiT1Lo9XVLE8Ct7goF1NlWdjw>

Matter Devices.

step-by-step instructions and visual assets (like LED pulse animations) for  
Setup and Reset modes. This  
update<https://c.gle/ADMV5J8lF491i1aCA733qz6qnYQiLKvgY2D0gFaY5d712QcUqP8hfV6L_WbQm-1aWCVoVwyg2w-RnPm2Jgj229Q8d6iEi7UPpyBjOv8KLK2k_R5eHKKVAODOurCJpnwBUZsaU_x1ZVUrkh4O4igPjbvjmgK6-fkl_Tai-MYlDwgtLgt1DjU>  
helps users confirm device behavior in real-time, leading to a 10% increase  
in commissioning success rates.

[Cloud] Secure your Cloud Run apps with on-click IAP integration.

Identity-aware proxy (IAP) gives you control over who can access your  
application, whether that’s inside or outside of your organization. You can  
now enable this for Cloud Run  
services<https://c.gle/ADMV5J_o4B2Ibvkx5XcI7rO5SkmT80BT7QMUgWsC6RPXxNV7uAyeVnIRSuQuaNJBjCyOQ8IgUz3sM1K4WpyfHhTxHb2zYhIHIxAfCSWxqLgwAoq2LwHyLqojokHsyLGUKRIoFFUji34KE8hTAlQRQv5axLQlRjPuDpftPm66DfMej2j07_Skcc0IB9DU3emk3Hpb1Y3wRHIk5aZRP2PfHtyBIvYjNmEgMif8kP_ZNKdCDG9r>  
with one-click in the console, a single gcloud CLI flag, or one Terraform  
configuration.

Bench.

We just delivered the first version of this leaderboard of LLMs for Android  
development. We used real-world Android development scenarios to ensure we  
had a realistic assessment. Check out the first  
results!<https://c.gle/ADMV5J9SbKKQKEfd9OaZ_M4DhpZqxC2IKBYepupwqKuAUpqqHKfEIzNrMDDUTXC3eHaW-ymVqyk94Sv-AUQdfRSW7wEYTVwqfueWnbYQCr38TekXw71tVGUblDW0pa_p6_WlAlqn5L8WgSGTej1h46CTIxyHxY1hn1UUdx3Rcz84GUpA3nHDAuslwBBQbDIG1KVR0R0ckY53Dlpyhi3Ne-W7SgYPEILbMIhZ>

capability for mobile devs is now available for those building Firebase web  
apps  
too.<https://c.gle/ADMV5J-HB3RoEKiH9oAYVcbbA-1oXJa41zSzHi1FEU_9muh9lkScEBbF9ACY5rXcuJ5lThNP5jYszcy5ZgYDJLhGSWPn59qUPys2pcYK8wDdUTovquvNKtqTwoqw23jjQv5m_ZAkBNetUWWho9VJvudJWe4PW5vmDN0XLxngu1E0bJhHJ4Gj0qSISddFlsF9nSK_DnaZpZ_dUmWFPwWNU1wE9QHd4w>

high-fidelity UIs in Stitch, and connect them to Antigravity using the

Start  
learning<https://c.gle/ADMV5J80bVCfx13PXaTHdXEToyTQzhczWy4-LB9DO4U_SQC4PCUV5SmFML-qqZOCFQKuR2T4arab1wf03i6fHdLeHerjpGGgwpdEc72CEF2WzSqoZkTJiYjHFcs7jsAaSCgkJ-mBvx0-fHMm9Vji_LKm1OW0WFBMmm7lAWKd2cxzBVvUWvnA6hwmK_v5dmXJ9s08tymgNMzi-PPl7ZB3v9dQ5g>

repoBrain.name: repo-brain
repoBrain.goal: Package execution results back into text, voice, and multimodal artifacts.

stages.label: Text Renderer
stages.purpose: Convert execution state into a user-facing text answer.

stages.label: TTS Renderer
stages.purpose: Turn the final text answer into voice when voice output is requested.

stages.label: Artifact Renderer
stages.purpose: Generate Excel, Word, PPT, image, or mixed multimodal output packages.

eventLedger.purpose: Keep event-sourced brain activity and execution traces.

profileMemory.purpose: Persistent agent cortex profile for mission, behavior, and specialization.

homeMemory.purpose: Household schedule/reminder/event memory reused by brains.

artifactStore.purpose: Persist generated documents, images, and exports.

supportStack.label: Event Sourcing
supportStack.purpose: Replayable brain state and specialization traces.

supportStack.label: Functional Model Router
supportStack.purpose: Assign models by role instead of by vendor.

supportStack.label: Policy Engine
supportStack.purpose: Guard network, artifact, and tool execution boundaries.

supportStack.label: Artifact Compiler
supportStack.purpose: Render text, voice, Excel, Word, and PPT outputs.

supportStack.label: Memory Adapters
supportStack.purpose: Leave clean interfaces for vector DB, RAG, reranking, and search layers.

technologyStack.label: Vector Database
technologyStack.purpose: Store semantic health records, report chunks, medicine history, hospital visits, and personal habits.

technologyStack.label: LangChain / LangGraph
technologyStack.purpose: Represent the taskflow as a graph with retries, checkpoints, and human follow-up nodes.
technologyStack.recommendedWhen: Use when execution units grow beyond a linear 5-stage taskflow.

technologyStack.label: RAG
technologyStack.purpose: Ground analysis on prior reports, medicine records, local notes, and retrieved medical references.

technologyStack.label: Elasticsearch
technologyStack.purpose: Index report text, OCR text, visit history, medicines, and external medical references for keyword + hybrid search.

technologyStack.label: Fine-Tuning
technologyStack.purpose: Only after enough confirmed report-analysis traces exist; not needed for the first working brain.

technologyStack.label: Agents
technologyStack.purpose: Treat each smart unit as the smallest execution cell: OCR agent, medical analyzer agent, follow-up interviewer agent, health archive agent.

technologyStack.label: LoRA
technologyStack.purpose: Optional later path to specialize a local medical analyzer without full fine-tuning.

technologyStack.label: Event Sourcing
technologyStack.purpose: Track every intake, analysis, follow-up, and advice event so the brain can evolve safely.

technologyStack.label: Structured Extraction
technologyStack.purpose: Turn messy hospital reports into structured fields such as hospital, date, department, metrics, medicine, next visit.

technologyStack.label: Policy Engine
technologyStack.purpose: Prevent unsafe medical claims, require disclaimers, and separate analysis from diagnosis.

technologyStack.label: Multimodal Index
technologyStack.purpose: Store OCR text plus report image linkage so future queries can trace back to the original scan.

steps.label: Input
steps.purpose: Accept text, voice, image, or mixed multimodal requests.

steps.label: Normalize
steps.purpose: Convert raw signals into one semantic command packet.

steps.label: Retrieve
steps.purpose: Check semantic memory, vector memory, JSON memory, and existing smart units.

steps.label: Decide
steps.purpose: Judge whether to reuse, extend, or autonomously create a smart unit.
steps.decisionMode: {'shouldCreateNewBrain': True, 'reason': 'Existing smart unit does not cover enough of the inferred requirement capabilities.'}

steps.label: Plan
steps.purpose: Compile an execution graph and assign the best models by function.

steps.label: Execute
steps.purpose: Invoke features, tools, searches, and agents until the taskflow completes.

steps.label: Render
steps.purpose: Return text, voice, and artifact outputs back to the user.

steps.label: Learn
steps.purpose: Write corrected understanding and execution evidence back into memory.

zones.label: Runtime
zones.purpose: Entry server, HTTP routes, dashboard assembly, voice loop, and network bridge.

zones.label: Cortex
zones.purpose: Brain design, taskflow compilation, requirement inference, and evolution memory.

zones.label: Features
zones.purpose: Execution surface used by smart units and runtime routing.

zones.label: Memory
zones.purpose: Persistent state, semantic examples, vector adapters, and household memory.

zones.label: Services
zones.purpose: Node or sidecar services that expand execution outside the runtime process.

zones.label: Current Smart Unit
zones.purpose: Current agent mission, feature bridge, and specialization status.

validation.goal: Validate that HomeHub reacts to a user demand, judges capability gaps on its own, and creates or reuses the right smart unit without relying on a fixed scenario template.

autonomousCreation.principle: HomeHub decides by itself whether an existing smart unit is enough or whether a new one must be created.
decisionFlow: infer capability requirements from the demand instead of from a fixed template
search existing smart units by semantic similarity and capability tags
reuse an existing unit if capability fit is high enough
create a new smart unit if capability fit is insufficient
expand the taskflow with retrieval, questioning, archive writing, and artifact generation only when the demand needs them

existingBrainCheck.expectedBehavior: HomeHub should check fit first, not assume that a new brain must always be created.

validationChain.name: Requirement Understanding
validationChain.whatHappens: The pre-brain converts the user demand into a normalized requirement packet with task type, input modes, desired outputs, and implied constraints.

validationChain.name: Capability Gap Analysis
validationChain.whatHappens: The exec-brain determines whether the current smart-unit library already covers the requirement well enough.

validationChain.name: Autonomous Smart-Unit Decision
validationChain.whatHappens: If fit is high, reuse an existing smart unit. If fit is low, HomeHub designs and creates a new one by itself.

validationChain.name: Taskflow Expansion
validationChain.whatHappens: HomeHub decides whether OCR, search, RAG, archive writing, follow-up questioning, and artifact generation are needed for this requirement.

validationChain.name: Execution and Packaging
validationChain.whatHappens: The selected or newly created unit executes the taskflow and the repo-brain packages the result into text, voice, and/or artifacts.

successCriteria: HomeHub does not depend on a hardcoded vertical scenario to decide what to build
the brain can infer capability requirements from the user's demand
existing smart units are searched before creation
new smart units are created only when the capability gap is real
optional behaviors such as search, archive building, and follow-up questioning are activated only when the requirement needs them

decision.reason: Existing smart unit does not cover enough of the inferred requirement capabilities.

explanations.router: Use the lightest local model for always-on intent triage.
explanations.semantic: Keep semantic normalization local to reduce latency and preserve privacy.
explanations.planner: Promote to a cloud planner only when the task needs network-backed deep planning or the cortex is highly specialized.
explanations.executor: Keep the main execution loop local-first so HomeHub behaves like a persistent box brain.
explanations.vision: Use local VL for default document/image understanding, and cloud multimodal only when richer network inference is allowed.
explanations.artifact: Route artifact and workflow generation to the coder model unless the task explicitly benefits from stronger cloud synthesis.
explanations.response: Use a smaller cloud model for polished responses only when the task already depends on the network.
explanations.embedding: Prefer multilingual embeddings for repo-brain memory and cross-lingual retrieval.
explanations.reranker: Rerank retrieved evidence before the exec-brain consumes it.
explanations.similarity: Use a compact multilingual similarity model to find matching brains, commands, and memory shards.
explanations.docMemory: Use multimodal memory indexing when the request contains images or document evidence.
explanations.audioReasoner: Reserve audio-text-to-text models for future richer voice reasoning instead of plain STT.

technologyStack.label: LangChain / LangGraph
technologyStack.purpose: Represent the taskflow as a graph with retries, checkpoints, and human follow-up nodes.

technologyStack.label: RAG

technologyStack.purpose: Index report text, OCR text, visit history, medicines, and external medical references for keyword + hybrid search.

technologyStack.label: Fine-Tuning
technologyStack.purpose: Only after enough confirmed report-analysis traces exist; not needed for the first working brain.

technologyStack.label: Agents
technologyStack.purpose: Treat each smart unit as the smallest execution cell: OCR agent, medical analyzer agent, follow-up interviewer agent, health archive agent.

technologyStack.label: LoRA
technologyStack.purpose: Optional later path to specialize a local medical analyzer without full fine-tuning.

technologyStack.label: Event Sourcing
technologyStack.purpose: Track every intake, analysis, follow-up, and advice event so the brain can evolve safely.

technologyStack.label: Structured Extraction
technologyStack.purpose: Turn messy hospital reports into structured fields such as hospital, date, department, metrics, medicine, next visit.

technologyStack.label: Policy Engine
technologyStack.purpose: Prevent unsafe medical claims, require disclaimers, and separate analysis from diagnosis.

technologyStack.label: Multimodal Index
technologyStack.purpose: Store OCR text plus report image linkage so future queries can trace back to the original scan.

validation.goal: Validate that HomeHub reacts to a user demand, judges capability gaps on its own, and creates or reuses the right smart unit without relying on a fixed scenario template.

autonomousCreation.principle: HomeHub decides by itself whether an existing smart unit is enough or whether a new one must be created.
decisionFlow: infer capability requirements from the demand instead of from a fixed template
search existing smart units by semantic similarity and capability tags
reuse an existing unit if capability fit is high enough
create a new smart unit if capability fit is insufficient
expand the taskflow with retrieval, questioning, archive writing, and artifact generation only when the demand needs them

existingBrainCheck.expectedBehavior: HomeHub should check fit first, not assume that a new brain must always be created.

validationChain.name: Requirement Understanding
validationChain.whatHappens: The pre-brain converts the user demand into a normalized requirement packet with task type, input modes, desired outputs, and implied constraints.

validationChain.name: Capability Gap Analysis
validationChain.whatHappens: The exec-brain determines whether the current smart-unit library already covers the requirement well enough.

validationChain.name: Autonomous Smart-Unit Decision
validationChain.whatHappens: If fit is high, reuse an existing smart unit. If fit is low, HomeHub designs and creates a new one by itself.

validationChain.name: Taskflow Expansion
validationChain.whatHappens: HomeHub decides whether OCR, search, RAG, archive writing, follow-up questioning, and artifact generation are needed for this requirement.

validationChain.name: Execution and Packaging
validationChain.whatHappens: The selected or newly created unit executes the taskflow and the repo-brain packages the result into text, voice, and/or artifacts.

successCriteria: HomeHub does not depend on a hardcoded vertical scenario to decide what to build
the brain can infer capability requirements from the user's demand
existing smart units are searched before creation
new smart units are created only when the capability gap is real
optional behaviors such as search, archive building, and follow-up questioning are activated only when the requirement needs them

blueprint.inputs: [{'name': '消费类型', 'type': 'string', 'description': '用户输入的消费类型，如衣服、餐饮等'}, {'name': '消费金额', 'type': 'number', 'description': '用户输入的消费金额'}, {'name': '图片内容', 'type': 'image', 'description': '用户提供的图片，用于识别消费金额'}]
blueprint.output: [{'name': '记录结果', 'type': 'string', 'description': '消费记录的结果，包括消费类型和金额'}, {'name': '每周总结', 'type': 'string', 'description': '每周六晚上六点发送的消费总结消息，包括总花费和详细消费情况'}]
blueprint.constraints: [{'condition': '无法判断是什么消费时', 'action': '用其他来记录'}, {'condition': '用户询问本周的消费总额和详细情况', 'action': '总结性告诉我花费总额和都花在什么地方了'}]

explanations.router: Use the lightest local model for always-on intent triage.
explanations.semantic: Keep semantic normalization local to reduce latency and preserve privacy.
explanations.planner: Promote to a cloud planner only when the task needs network-backed deep planning or the cortex is highly specialized.
explanations.executor: Keep the main execution loop local-first so HomeHub behaves like a persistent box brain.
explanations.vision: Use local VL for default document/image understanding, and cloud multimodal only when richer network inference is allowed.
explanations.artifact: Route artifact and workflow generation to the coder model unless the task explicitly benefits from stronger cloud synthesis.
explanations.response: Use a smaller cloud model for polished responses only when the task already depends on the network.
explanations.embedding: Prefer multilingual embeddings for repo-brain memory and cross-lingual retrieval.
explanations.reranker: Rerank retrieved evidence before the exec-brain consumes it.
explanations.similarity: Use a compact multilingual similarity model to find matching brains, commands, and memory shards.
explanations.docMemory: Use multimodal memory indexing when the request contains images or document evidence.
explanations.audioReasoner: Reserve audio-text-to-text models for future richer voice reasoning instead of plain STT.

validation.goal: Validate that HomeHub reacts to a user demand, judges capability gaps on its own, and creates or reuses the right smart unit without relying on a fixed scenario template.

autonomousCreation.principle: HomeHub decides by itself whether an existing smart unit is enough or whether a new one must be created.
decisionFlow: infer capability requirements from the demand instead of from a fixed template
search existing smart units by semantic similarity and capability tags
reuse an existing unit if capability fit is high enough
create a new smart unit if capability fit is insufficient
expand the taskflow with retrieval, questioning, archive writing, and artifact generation only when the demand needs them

existingBrainCheck.expectedBehavior: HomeHub should check fit first, not assume that a new brain must always be created.

validationChain.name: Requirement Understanding
validationChain.whatHappens: The pre-brain converts the user demand into a normalized requirement packet with task type, input modes, desired outputs, and implied constraints.

validationChain.name: Capability Gap Analysis
validationChain.whatHappens: The exec-brain determines whether the current smart-unit library already covers the requirement well enough.

validationChain.name: Autonomous Smart-Unit Decision
validationChain.whatHappens: If fit is high, reuse an existing smart unit. If fit is low, HomeHub designs and creates a new one by itself.

validationChain.name: Taskflow Expansion
validationChain.whatHappens: HomeHub decides whether OCR, search, RAG, archive writing, follow-up questioning, and artifact generation are needed for this requirement.

validationChain.name: Execution and Packaging
validationChain.whatHappens: The selected or newly created unit executes the taskflow and the repo-brain packages the result into text, voice, and/or artifacts.

successCriteria: HomeHub does not depend on a hardcoded vertical scenario to decide what to build
the brain can infer capability requirements from the user's demand
existing smart units are searched before creation
new smart units are created only when the capability gap is real
optional behaviors such as search, archive building, and follow-up questioning are activated only when the requirement needs them
